articles.title: Smiling Through The Pain — See Also
articles.content:
保罗·韦斯再失两位诉讼合伙人：他们坚称一切安好。

好吧，看来现在就让AI来做招聘吧：Revolut的“颠覆性”技术采用正被嘲笑。

宽带费用贵？都怪移民！：特朗普想把宽带成本上涨归咎于移民欺诈。

大律所宣布合伙人奖金池：德杰律师事务所赚得盆满钵满！

又一起社交媒体言论自由逮捕事件：这次，德州警方表明他们不在乎第一修正案。

本文《微笑着承受痛苦——另见》首发于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Paul, Weiss Loses Two More Litigation Partners: They insist everything is still alright.

Sure, Just Let AI Do The Hiring Now I Guess: Revolut’s “disruptive” tech adoption is getting ridiculed.

Expensive Internet? Blame The Migrants!: Trump wants to pin broadband cost increases on immigrant fraud.

Biglaw Firm Announces Partner Bonus Pool: Debevoise & Plimpton is swimming in the money!

Another Free Speech Social Media Arrest: This time, Texas police show they don’t care about the First Amendment.

The post Smiling Through The Pain — See Also appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Payday For General Counsel Is Impressive
articles.content:
编者注：欢迎来到我们的每日专栏每日趣味问答！

根据BarkerGilmore的2026年内部法律顾问薪酬报告，大型上市公司总法律顾问的中位总薪酬是多少？

提示：前四分位的收入超过500万美元。

答案见下一页。

本文《总法律顾问的薪酬令人印象深刻》首次发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Ed. Note: Welcome to our daily feature Trivia Question of the Day!

According to BarkerGilmore’s 2026 In-House Counsel Compensation Report, what is the median total compensation for General Counsel at large public companies?

articles.title: How Appealing Weekly Roundup
articles.content:
编者按：以下是霍华德·巴什曼（Howard Bashman）"How Appealing"博客的每周精选摘要，该博客是首个专注于上诉诉讼的网络博客。欲了解更多故事，请访问How Appealing。

“有丑闻。有法律。然后还有这个。”艾米丽·巴泽隆（Emily Bazelon）和大卫·弗伦奇（David French）在《纽约时报》在线进行了这场对话。

“纽约州高等法院与沃尔科夫就仇恨言论法要求的博弈”：彭博法律频道的贝丝·王（Beth Wang）对此进行了报道（完整访问需要订阅）。

“特朗普把我们带到了这个国家从未去过的地方”：诺亚·沙赫特曼（Noah Shachtman）在《纽约时报》发表了这篇客座文章。

“最高法院驳回涉及智障人士的死刑案件；多数意见以程序理由驳回了约瑟夫·克里夫顿·史密斯（Joseph Clifton Smith）的案件，其智力能力刚好处于可免除死刑的门槛边缘”：贾斯汀·乔维纳尔（Justin Jouvenal）在《华盛顿邮报》对此进行了报道。

“特朗普家族的审计豁免权让国税局陷入两难；联邦法律禁止国税局在总统或其助手的指示下停止审计”：安德鲁·杜伦（Andrew Duehren）在《纽约时报》发表了这篇新闻分析。

“在全美各州议会长期搁置后，一种限制政治资金的新方法在夏威夷成为法律；SB 2471法案的制定旨在允许该州遏制企业政治支出”：威尔·列侬（Will Lennon）和比阿特丽斯·彼得森（Beatrice Peterson）在ABC新闻对此进行了报道。

本文首次发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Ed. Note: A weekly roundup of just a few items from Howard Bashman’s How Appealing blog, the Web’s first blog devoted to appellate litigation. Check out these stories and more at How Appealing.

“There Are Scandals. There Is the Law. And Then There’s This.” Emily Bazelon and David French have this conversation online at The New York Times.

“NY High Court, Volokh Tussle With Hate Speech Law’s Requirements”: Beth Wang of Bloomberg Law has this report (subscription required for full access).

“Trump Just Took Us Somewhere the Country Has Never Been Before”: Noah Shachtman has this guest essay online at The New York Times.

“Supreme Court dismisses death penalty case on people with mental disabilities; The majority dismissed on procedural grounds the case of Joseph Clifton Smith, whose mental faculties skirt the threshold for being spared the death penalty”: Justin Jouvenal of The Washington Post has this report.

“Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind; Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides”: Andrew Duehren of The New York Times has this news analysis.

“After languishing in state legislatures across the country, a novel approach to curtailing money in politics becomes law in Hawaii; SB 2471 was crafted allow the state to curb corporate political spending”: Will Lennon and Beatrice Peterson of ABC News have this report.

The post How Appealing Weekly Roundup appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: When Did The First Amendment Become A Money Printer?
articles.content:
做好准备。这个案情真是荒唐。

在田纳西州，一位前警长最近获得了80多万美元的和解金，原因是他的言论自由权利受到了侵犯。警察在他家中逮捕了他，并将其监禁了一个多月，原因仅仅是他发布了一张查理·柯克的表情包。这从一开始就是一个板上钉钉的案件，但看到事情以这种方式发展，应该给全国各地的警察部门一个警示：你不能仅仅因为别人说了你不喜欢的话就逮捕他们。

如果德州的警察愿意读一读相关案例就好了。

德州一名女子最近因警告当地居民水龙头流出的水受到污染而被逮捕。这不是那种关于5G污染水库的阴谋论——那水看起来是这样的：

Fox新闻有相关报道：

位于亨德森县、距达拉斯东南约一小时车程的特立尼达市警察，于5月8日逮捕了詹妮弗·科姆斯，并指控她犯有重罪——虚假警报或报告。

她的逮捕源于在个人Facebook账号"Southern Belle Watch"上发布的一篇帖子，她在帖子中声称该市的用水问题已导致多人因细菌感染住院。

指控的理由是，她的帖子包含可能在该社区引发恐惧、恐慌或不必要紧急响应的虚假信息。真好知道他们有这样的法律规定——为什么人们在否定疫苗有效性、直到儿童因麻疹死亡才不得不听从科学的时候，没有动用这些法律呢？即使她对原因的说法在事实上是错误的，她的发帖也不可能比人们打开水龙头看到那些东西时产生的恐惧和恐慌更多。

她随后提起了诉讼，要求对这次逮捕进行赔偿。她不太可能从这个诉讼中获得六位数的赔偿——与那位前警长被关押37天相比，她只被关了一天——但无论怎样，她应该因为时间的损失和权利被侵犯而获得赔偿。

相关链接：女子因在Facebook上发布关于特立尼达供水问题的帖子被逮捕后提起诉讼 [Fox新闻]

早些时候：因发布查理·柯克帖子而被逮捕的男子赢得六位数和解金

克里斯·威廉姆斯于2021年6月成为Above the Law的社交媒体经理和副编辑。在加入该团队之前，他曾在Facebook群组"法律学校表情包——面向刺头T14法学院"中兼职担任初级表情包领主™。他在密苏里州熬到从圣路易斯华盛顿大学法学院毕业。他曾是一名造船工人，现在正在学习游泳，对修辞学、斯宾诺莎主义者和幽默感兴趣。重新开始骑自行车也不会有什么坏处。你可以通过电子邮件christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com或Tweet/Bluesky @WritesForRent联系他。

《第一修正案何时变成了印钞机？》一文首次发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Brace yourself. This fact pattern is stupid.

In Tennessee, a former sheriff recently earned an $800k+ settlement after his free speech rights were infringed. Police arrested him at his home and jailed him for over a month because he posted a Charlie Kirk meme. It was a cut-and-dried case from the start, but seeing it play out this way should have served as a reminder to police departments nationwide that you can’t just arrest people because they say things you don’t like.

If only the police in Texas bothered to read.

A woman in Texas was recently arrested for warning the locals that the water coming from their faucets was contaminated. And this wasn’t one of those conspiratorial 5G has contaminated our reservoirs either — the water looked like this:

Fox has coverage:

Police in the City of Trinidad, located in Henderson County around an hour south-east of Dallas, arrested Jennifer Combs on May 8 and charged her with felony false alarm or report.

Her arrest stems from a Facebook post she made on her ‘Southern Belle Watch’ account, where she claimed that the city’s water issues had led to hospitalizations due to bacteria.

The rationale for the charge was that her posts involved false information that could create fear, panic, or unnecessary emergency response in the community. Nice to know they have laws like that on the books — why aren’t they brought up when people were denying vaccine efficacy so hard that children had to die from measles before they listened to science again? Even if she were factually wrong about the cause, there’s no way her posting caused more fear and panic than people turning on their sinks and seeing what they saw.

She’s since filed a lawsuit for being arrested. It isn’t likely that she will get six figures from the suit — she only spent a day in jail compared to the former sheriff’s 37, but she should be compensated for her time and the violation of her rights either way.

Woman Files Lawsuit After Arrest For Facebook Post Concerning Trinidad Water Supply Issues [Fox]

Earlier: Man Arrested For Charlie Kirk Post Wins 6 Figure Settlement

Chris Williams became a social media manager and assistant editor for Above the Law in June 2021. Prior to joining the staff, he moonlighted as a minor Memelord™ in the Facebook group Law School Memes for Edgy T14s .  He endured Missouri long enough to graduate from Washington University in St. Louis School of Law. He is a former boat builder who is learning to swim and is interested in rhetoric, Spinozists and humor. Getting back in to cycling wouldn’t hurt either. You can reach him by email at christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com and by Tweet/Bluesky at @WritesForRent.

The post When Did The First Amendment Become A Money Printer? appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Something Just Happened At The Supreme Court That Has NEVER Happened Before
articles.content:
如果你不熟悉“Scorigami”这一体育现象，这里指的是某项体育比赛中首次出现的最终比分。例如，NFL比赛是否曾以40-40平局结束？答案是肯定的，这在去年9月28日首次发生于绿湾和达拉斯的比赛中。

去年，NFL出现了四个此前从未有过的最终比分。

现在有一个追踪最高法院“SCOTUSgami”的推特账号——这是指某个特定大法官组合的判决首次出现。这周就发生了这种情况。

SCOTUSgami的规则与体育版略有不同。该账号的作者并未考虑所有投票，因此这里Kagan的单独异议不会影响SCOTUSgami的统计。关于这一过程还有更多内容...

扩展到完整的法官组合会很有趣，例如Sotomayor的协同意见书仅由Kavanaugh联名签署，这与仅有Sotomayor的协同意见书不同。这确实会创造更多的SCOTUSgami结果——该账号明确表示“它们不应太频繁”，但任何-gami的特征是初期会有很多这样的结果，然后逐渐减少。

总之，对于关注这一机构衰落的书呆子们来说，这是一个有趣的新消遣。

这篇文章首次发表于Above the Law，标题为《最高法院刚刚发生了一件前所未有的事情》。
articles.summary:
If you’re not familiar with the sports phenomenon of “Scorigami,” it describes a situation where teams set a final score that has never happened before in the history of the sport. For instance, has an NFL game ever ended in a 40-40 tie? The answer is yes, and it happened for the first time last year when Green Bay and Dallas tied on September 28th.

There were four final scores in the NFL last year that had never occurred before.

Now there’s a Twitter account tracking SCOTUSgami — the first time a decision features a particular lineup of justices. And it happened this week.

The guidelines for SCOTUSgami are a little different than the sports version. The author isn’t taking all the votes into account, meaning Kagan’s lone dissent here has no bearing on the SCOTUSgami. But a concurrence written by Sotomayor and joined only by Kavanaugh counts. A little more about the process…

It would be interesting to expand the -gami out to full lineups, for instance recognizing the 6-3 of a Sotomayor, joined by Kavanaugh concurrence with Kagan dissent as distinct from just a Sotomayor, joined by Kavanaugh concurrence alone. It would certainly create a lot more SCOTUSgami results at first — and the account is clear that “they shouldn’t be too frequent,” but the thing about any -gami is that there will be a lot of them at first, gradually petering out.

In any event, a fun new distraction for nerds watching the institution fall.

Joe Patrice is a senior editor at Above the Law and co-host of Thinking Like A Lawyer. Feel free to email any tips, questions, or comments. Follow him on Twitter or Bluesky if you’re interested in law, politics, and a healthy dose of college sports news.

The post Something Just Happened At The Supreme Court That Has NEVER Happened Before appeared first on Above the Law.
